title: Episode 15 - Season 2 Opener | Back For More
summary: Season Two is officially underway and The Outside Line is back for more. 🔥 We kick off the new season by reviewing Round 1 of the 2026 World Superbike Championship, coming from the iconic Phillip Island Circuit in Australia. From early-season form to standout performances in the wet, we break down what we learned from the opening round of the year.  We also dive into the headline making decision to move away from Phillip Island and onto a brand-new Australian street circuit from 2027, and what that could mean for riders, teams and fans.  There's plenty more from the wider paddock too, with the latest news and talking points from MotoGP, British Superbikes & AMA.  And of course, it wouldn't be OSL without a bit of jeopardy. Jack puts Whit to the test in Jack Track Trivia, this time all about Phillip Island, before firing listener questions straight at him in a no-holds barred Presser Q&A Session.  New Season, Same Full-Throttle Energy / Mayhem.  Let's Go 🏁 Enjoying the Show? Support: 🚨OSL YouTube Membership - £2.99 / Month🚨 Want more Outside Line and less waiting around? Join the OSL YouTube Membership and get closer to the podcast than ever.  What you get:  + Early access to ad-free episodes - watch 24 hours before public release +  Bonus behind-the-scenes content you won't see anywhere else + Presser Q&A Shoutout in the show when you join + Priority Presser Q&A questions - your questions jump the long queue + Monthly prize draw entry to come and watch a live recording at Huddersfield International No fluff. No nonsense. Just more access, more content, and more chances to be part of the show. title: Episode 14 - 'Take The Piss!' - Q&A UNFILTERED
summary: Episode 14 is all about you.  We throw the pit board out to the listeners and answer your questions.  The serious ones, the spicy ones, and the ones that probably shouldn't have been sent.... but we're glad they were.  From racing insights and paddock stories to behind-the-scenes chaos, opinions we'll probably regret, and a few curveballs we didn't see coming, this episode is a proper deep dive into what you actually want to know . No scripts, no filters, just honest answers, good laughs, and abit of mayhem along the way.  Thanks to every one who sent questions in. If yours didn't make it to air this time, it's staying in the vault for the next one! And please do keep the questions coming, email: theoutsidelinepodcast@gmail.com Enjoying the Show? title: Episode 13 - 'Behind the Broadcast' - Richard Coventry: BSB TV Director
summary: What actually goes into putting British Superbike racing on your TV screens every race weekend? In Episode 13 of The Outside Line, we head behind the broadcast with Richard Coventry, the BSB Race & Presentation TV Director and the man responsible for turning flat-out racing into the live coverage we all watch at home. From camera placement and director calls to live decision-making, storytelling, and capturing the moments that matter, Cov breaks down how a BSB race weekend is broadcast, and what viewers never get to see or hear. We explore the pressure of live television, how races are shaped for the audience, working with commentators and production crews, and how modern broadcast tech has changed the way motorsport is consumed. If you’ve ever wondered how the racing you love actually makes it to air, this is the ultimate TV broadcast episode. 🎥 Behind-the-scenes insights 🏁 Live race direction & storytelling 🎙 Working with commentators & crews 📡 The tech that brings BSB to life A must-listen for motorsport fans, media nerds, and anyone curious about what happens beyond the pit wall. Enjoying the Show? title: Episode 12 - Xmas Special - 'Wheelie Neilly!'
summary: Jack & James are joined by racing royalty and three-time British Superbike Champion🏆 Niall Mackenzie for a special Christmas edition of 'The Outside Line'.  Niall and James reminisce about their days battling for a championship onboard the Cadbury's Boost as former teammates, sharing untold stories from the paddock and reliving some properly spicy moments from their racing careers. The conversation then shifts to Niall's no-nonsense take on racing across the respected championships this season, with sharp insights from one of Britain's most respected voices in motorsport. title: Episode 11 - Kyle Ryde 'Youth'
summary: Jack & James are joined by two-time British Superbike champion Kyle Ryde for a deep dive into his incredible season - thanks to Whit pulling in a favour to get him on the show just hours before he departs for some winter testing in Spain.  Kyle breaks down the high, lows, and defining moments that shaped his championship campaign, before looking ahead to an exciting 2026 as he prepares to take on the challenge of the new Ducati. He also answers a stack of viewer / listener questions, covering everything from preparation and mindset to golf and snooker technique!  A must listen for BSB fans, rider nerds, and anyone who loves hearing riders in full, unfiltered mode.  Support: If you want to support the podcast and look for some top quality gear, use the affiliate link below to shop some of our favourites from Red Torpedo.  https://www.redtorpedo.com/collections/the-outside-line Enjoying the show? title: Episode 10 - Lee Johnston  'A Gloomy little man!'
summary: Jack & James return to the sofa fresh off the back of working at Motorcycle Live. This week they are  joined by one of the sport's fan favourite racers - multiple North West 200 Winner, Isle of Man TT victor, and BSB Supersport contender, Lee "The General" Johnston.  Lee opens up about his battle back from injury, the realities of recovery, and how it's shaped his mindset heading into 2026. He reveals his plans for next season and the trio dig into the current health of the sport we all love.  Expect honest insight, sharp opinions, plenty of laughs, and whether you're a die-hard road racing devotee or a BSB enthusiast, this is high-octane listening you don't want to miss. title: Episode 9 - 'The Chequered Flag' / + Studio Guest: Matt Roberts
summary: 🎙️ Season Finale I Full Throttle to the Finish: Jerez & Brands Hatch Breakdown with special guest Matt Roberts. It's the grand finale to the season! Jack & James go flat-out as they dissect a huge weekend of championship deciding racing, from the dramatic World Superbike showdown at Jerez to the British Superbike decider at Brands Hatch.  They're joined by TNT Sports front man and BSB anchor Matt Roberts, who brings insider perspective from both sides of the mic, sharing stories from the grid and what it's really like covering the sport from the sharp end.  Plus the lads wrap up the year with a two part "Presser Q&A" segment, taking your listener questions head on, no soft passes, no holding back. It's the perfect send off to a wild season of racing, banter, and behind-the-scenes insight. Strap in, this one's going full throttle.  Support: Enjoying the show? title: Episode 7 - Whitham Call-Out! ✊🏻"
summary: The boys are back in the hangar! In this week's episode of The Outside Line, Jack and James dive into all the latest drama and standout moments from the World and British Superbike Championships. Whit is on fiery form as he calls out a few old racing legends for a comeback showdown, and shares a wild story involving an aerial acrobatic team flying upside down over a live grid! In Rev's Roulette, the ante's been raised with the addition of World Superbike onboard laps to the mix to make things even more challenging! There also a double serving of Presser Q&A this week, with the lads tackling your listener questions.  It's fast, funny, and full of the kind of chaos only The Outside Line can deliver. title: Episode 5 - "Phone-Gate!"
summary: In this action-packed episode, we dive into the highs, lows, and outright madness of the Thruxton and Cadwell rounds of British Superbikes. Whit shares the legendary “phone gate” story – yes, a mobile phone really did take a trip down a portaloo – and we talk generator failures that caused plenty of headaches on and off the track. We also discuss the big announcement of Jonathan Rea’s retirement at the end of the season and what it means for the world of Superbike racing. Plus, we’re joined on the sofa by our first ever guest, bringing a fresh perspective to the chaos and excitement. And don’t miss a special announcement about our podcast sponsorship, which could change the way you tune in forever! Strap in, because this episode has everything from laughs to insight, and a few jaw-dropping moments you won’t want to miss.
